title: dryopteris filix-mas subsp. pseudorigida
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. pseudorigida, also known as the stiff-leaved male fern, is a subspecies of the common male fern, characterized by its stiff, upright fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the fronds of the stiff-leaved male fern are often dried, ground into a powder, or infused in hot water to create teas, decoctions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
